unified_customers.design_reason: Present with high confidence in all customer datasets and required as the core customer-level key.

unified_customers.description: Customer full name as a single displayable string.
unified_customers.source_coverage: A, B, C (A and B directly provide full_name; C can derive it by combining first_name and last_name)
unified_customers.design_reason: Keep a unified full_name for broadest cross-client compatibility. Client C has separate first_name and last_name, but A and B only provide full names, so full_name should be the canonical shared field.
Included explicitly because one source provides structured name components. It should remain nullable since A and B do not provide reliable first-name parsing from full_name.
Included explicitly because one source provides structured surname data. It should remain nullable since A and B do not provide reliable last-name parsing from full_name.
Available across all customer datasets and valuable for customer identity resolution and communication.

unified_customers.description: Customer phone number in source-provided format.
unified_customers.design_reason: Available across all customer datasets, but stored as string to preserve international formatting and source variability.

unified_customers.description: Date the customer registered or signed up.
unified_customers.design_reason: Present in all customer datasets. Use date as the target type, with parsing/quality handling because some source values are malformed or inconsistently formatted.
Include city because geography is useful at customer level and one client provides it explicitly. Nullable because A and C do not contain a customer-city field.

unified_customers.description: Customer country, preferably as a standard country code when available.
unified_customers.design_reason: Include country because geography should support both city and country where available. Nullable because A and B do not contain a customer-country field.

unified_customers.description: Identifier of the originating client dataset for the customer record.
unified_customers.source_coverage: A, B, C (derived from dataset context)
unified_customers.design_reason: Needed for lineage, troubleshooting, and preserving source provenance across integrated customer records.

unified_orders.design_reason: Present with high confidence in all order datasets and required as the core order-level key.

unified_orders.description: Identifier of the customer associated with the order.
unified_orders.design_reason: Present in all order datasets and required to relate orders to unified_customers.
Present in all order datasets. Use date as the target type, with validation and parsing rules because some source values are malformed or use different date formats.

unified_orders.description: Original order monetary amount in the source currency.
unified_orders.design_reason: All sources provide an order total. Preserve the original numeric value as the canonical source amount.

unified_orders.description: Original currency code for the order amount.
unified_orders.source_coverage: B, C directly; A not explicitly provided
unified_orders.design_reason: Monetary values should preserve original currency. Even though client A lacks an explicit currency field, the unified schema should retain currency to support multi-currency processing.

unified_orders.description: Order amount normalized to USD using an agreed exchange-rate process.
unified_orders.source_coverage: None directly; derived for A, B, C when currency and exchange rates are available
unified_orders.design_reason: Include a normalized USD amount in addition to original amount and currency to enable cross-client analytics. It is nullable because it must be derived and may be unavailable when source currency or conversion rates are missing.

unified_orders.description: Order lifecycle status in source or mapped standardized form.
unified_orders.design_reason: Present in all order datasets and useful for operational and analytical reporting, though multilingual values may require mapping to a controlled vocabulary.

unified_orders.description: Shipping or delivery address associated with the order.
unified_orders.design_reason: Present in all order datasets and correctly belongs at the order level because shipment destinations can vary across orders for the same customer.

unified_orders.description: Identifier of the originating client dataset for the order record.
unified_orders.source_coverage: A, B, C (derived from dataset context)
unified_orders.design_reason: Needed for lineage, traceability, and handling source-specific transformations in the integrated orders table.
